role.name: Back Office Executive
role.description: Protect business workflows through clean, accurate data.

chapters.title: Excel Sheet of Doom
chapters.story: You receive 500 customer records. Some names have extra spaces, emails are missing, dates are wrong, and one customer name is literally “Test Test Please Ignore.”

outcome.title: Printer Possession Ending
outcome.description: System sends invoice to vendor, birthday wish to finance, and printer starts printing dance steps.
outcome.moralLesson: Small data mistakes create big business problems.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Protect confidential data, verify duplicates, and keep a traceable correction record.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Small data mistakes create big business problems.

choices.text: Clean duplicates, validate mandatory fields, check dates

outcome.title: Data Hero Ending
outcome.description: Upload succeeds. Manager says, “Aaj Excel ne tumhe nahi, tumne Excel ko control kiya.”

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: acceptable start, but explain the trade-off and document what you checked.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Protect confidential data, verify duplicates, and keep a traceable correction record.

outcome.title: Vanishing Customer Ending
outcome.description: Half the customers disappear. Sales team starts searching like CID episode.
outcome.moralLesson: Never delete business data without confirmation.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Protect confidential data, verify duplicates, and keep a traceable correction record.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Never delete business data without confirmation.

miniGame.title: Clean the Excel Sheet of Doom
miniGame.instructions: Select the rows that must be cleaned before upload.
miniGame.prompt: The upload portal rejects messy records. Identify rows that need correction.
miniGame.hint: Look for missing email, duplicate employee ID, and impossible date.

options.text: Employee 1043 has name, email, and valid joining date

miniGame.successMessage: Data cleaned! Excel stopped screaming and the upload button became friendly again.
miniGame.failureMessage: Messy data uploaded. The server coughed, sneezed, and generated 407 error emails.

chapters.learningObjective: Practice duplicate check workflow for a Back Office Executive situation involving data accuracy and validation.
safetyGuardrails: Protect personal data and confidential documents.
Do not process duplicate/unclear records without verification.
Keep audit trail for every correction.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: Use the duplicate check workflow to balance professional judgment with constraints instead of guessing under pressure.
chapters.safeExplanation: Operations simulation: protect private data, verify unclear records, and keep an audit trail. This chapter is educational and does not replace qualified professional review.
chapters.mentorFeedback: Operations Mentor says: At beginner level, show your evidence, explain the trade-off, and follow the duplicate check workflow before choosing speed.

guardrails: Protect personal data and confidential documents.
Do not process duplicate/unclear records without verification.
Keep audit trail for every correction.
safetyReview.notes: Phase 23 metadata seed. Requires human review before production publishing changes.

chapters.title: Invoice Avalanche
chapters.story: A stack of invoices arrives. Some totals mismatch, one vendor GST number is missing, and one invoice is clearly a restaurant bill.

outcome.title: Finance Thunder Ending
outcome.description: Finance calls immediately. The restaurant bill is now waiting for project approval.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Protect confidential data, verify duplicates, and keep a traceable correction record.

choices.text: Validate vendor details, totals, dates, and approval requirements

outcome.title: Clean Processing Ending
outcome.description: Only correct invoices move forward. Finance sends a thumbs-up instead of a thunderstorm.
outcome.moralLesson: Back office quality protects business records.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: acceptable start, but explain the trade-off and document what you checked.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Protect confidential data, verify duplicates, and keep a traceable correction record.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Back office quality protects business records.

choices.text: Guess missing details from old files

outcome.title: Assumption Audit Ending
outcome.description: Audit asks for proof. Your answer is “I felt it was correct.” Audit does not enjoy feelings.
outcome.moralLesson: Missing data should be clarified, not guessed.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Protect confidential data, verify duplicates, and keep a traceable correction record.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Missing data should be clarified, not guessed.

chapters.learningObjective: Practice privacy filter workflow for a Back Office Executive situation involving document accuracy and approval flow.
safetyGuardrails: Protect personal data and confidential documents.
Do not process duplicate/unclear records without verification.
Keep audit trail for every correction.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: Use the privacy filter workflow to balance professional judgment with constraints instead of guessing under pressure.
chapters.safeExplanation: Operations simulation: protect private data, verify unclear records, and keep an audit trail. This chapter is educational and does not replace qualified professional review.
chapters.mentorFeedback: Operations Mentor says: At intermediate level, show your evidence, explain the trade-off, and follow the privacy filter workflow before choosing speed.

guardrails: Protect personal data and confidential documents.
Do not process duplicate/unclear records without verification.
Keep audit trail for every correction.
safetyReview.notes: Phase 23 metadata seed. Requires human review before production publishing changes.

chapters.title: Mail Merge Madness
chapters.story: You must send 300 customer emails. One wrong column mapping can call everyone “Dear Outstanding Payment.”

outcome.title: Dear Chaos Ending
outcome.description: Customers receive wrong names, wrong amounts, and one person gets called “Test User.”
outcome.moralLesson: Bulk communication needs careful preview and validation.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Protect confidential data, verify duplicates, and keep a traceable correction record.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Bulk communication needs careful preview and validation.

choices.text: Preview sample rows, verify fields, check recipients, and send test mail

outcome.title: Safe Send Ending
outcome.description: Emails go correctly, privacy is protected, and nobody receives a bill for another customer.
outcome.moralLesson: Testing applies to office workflows too.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Protect confidential data, verify duplicates, and keep a traceable correction record.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Testing applies to office workflows too.

outcome.title: Privacy Parade Ending
outcome.description: All customer emails are visible. Legal team enters the chat at running speed.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Protect confidential data, verify duplicates, and keep a traceable correction record.
